title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in Corpus Christi, Texas
twitter: 🚛 Semi crash in Corpus Christi? The truck's BLACK BOX data can DISAPPEAR within days. Don't wait. Call 855-WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) NOW to connect with a truck accident attorney fast. ⏱️ https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/black-box-data-after-a-semi-crash-in-corpus-christi-texas
linkedin:
If you handle truck accident litigation in Texas, you already know: black box data is often the most powerful evidence in a semi-truck crash case — and it has a very short window before it's overwritten or destroyed.

After a collision in Corpus Christi, a commercial truck's Electronic Control Module (ECM) and Electronic Logging Device (ELD) can capture speed, braking, hours of service, and driver behavior in the seconds before impact. That data can be the difference between a strong case and an uphill battle.

The problem? Trucking companies and their insurers move fast. Preservation letters need to go out immediately, and the attorney on the case needs to understand federal FMCSA regulations, spoliation arguments, and how to compel production before data is lost.

WreckMatch connects injured victims with attorneys who specialize in exactly this — commercial truck accidents in Texas, with the urgency these cases demand.

reddit_title: Semi truck hit me in Corpus Christi, TX — does the truck's black box data actually help my case and how do I make sure it's preserved?
reddit_body:
Black box data (from the truck's ECM and ELD) is often the most critical evidence in a semi-truck accident case. It can record vehicle speed, hard braking events, throttle position, engine RPMs, and hours-of-service logs in the moments before a crash. In Texas, this kind of data has been used to prove driver fatigue, speeding, and negligent behavior that might otherwise be impossible to demonstrate.

The catch is that this data can be overwritten in as little as 30 days depending on the system, and trucking companies have every incentive to let that happen. The very first thing an attorney should do is send a spoliation letter — a formal legal demand to preserve all electronic data, dashcam footage, driver logs, and maintenance records. If the company destroys evidence after receiving that letter, it can seriously damage their case in court.

If you were hurt in a Corpus Christi semi crash and you're trying to figure out next steps, don't wait on this one. Finding an attorney who handles commercial truck accidents specifically (not just general personal injury) makes a big difference here. A site like wreckmatch.com can help match you with attorneys who focus on exactly these kinds of cases in Texas.
